rows.name: Elbow Drop
rows.notes: Can only be performed from Forward Jump; spike KD vs. airborne opponents; stops Kimberly's air momentum (useful for baiting anti-airs); maximum advantage is only possible when input at the latest possible timing (frame 30 of jump; otherwise, connects with 2nd to last active frame); minimum advantage is more likely to apply to tall standing characters, but is usually at least +5/-2

raw.notes: Can only be performed from Forward Jump; spike KD vs. airborne opponents; stops Kimberly's air momentum (useful for baiting anti-airs); maximum advantage is only possible when input at the latest possible timing (frame 30 of jump; otherwise, connects with 2nd to last active frame); minimum advantage is more likely to apply to tall standing characters, but is usually at least {{sf6-adv|VP|+5}}/{{sf6-adv|M|-2}}

rows.name: Step Up (neutral hop)
rows.notes: Startup refers to earliest frame that Kimberly can perform air follow-ups after Hop

raw.notes: Startup refers to earliest frame that Kimberly can perform air follow-ups after Hop

rows.name: Step Up (forward hop)
rows.notes: Crosses over the opponent; startup refers to earliest frame that Kimberly can perform air follow-ups after Hop

raw.notes: Crosses over the opponent; startup refers to earliest frame that Kimberly can perform air follow-ups after Hop

rows.name: Step Up (backward hop)
rows.notes: Startup refers to earliest frame that Kimberly can perform air follow-ups after Hop

raw.notes: Startup refers to earliest frame that Kimberly can perform air follow-ups after Hop

rows.name: Torso Cleaver
rows.notes: Kicking legs are Anti-Air Invuln 18-27f (cannot hit cross-up); active frames 3-7 only hit airborne opponents above Kimberly (only active frames 1-2 are useful for meaty pressure); puts opponent into limited juggle state; () refers to higher KD advantage when juggled into

raw.notes: Kicking legs are Anti-Air Invuln 18-27f (cannot hit cross-up); active frames 3-7 only hit airborne opponents above Kimberly (only active frames 1-2 are useful for meaty pressure); puts opponent into limited juggle state; () refers to higher KD advantage when juggled into

rows.name: Shadow Slide
rows.notes: 6-21f low profile and upper body projectile invuln (only on the Slide itself); puts opponent into limited juggle state; advantage varies significantly depending on which active frame connects; () refers to KD advantage when juggled into (KD +47 from 5MP~HP unless delayed); cancel to SA3 can whiff when used from longer range

raw.notes: 6-21f low profile and upper body projectile invuln (only on the Slide itself); puts opponent into limited juggle state; advantage varies significantly depending on which active frame connects; () refers to KD advantage when juggled into (KD +47 from 5MP~HP unless delayed); cancel to SA3 can whiff when used from longer range

rows.name: Neck Hunter
rows.notes: SA3 cancel can only combo in high connect juggles, or if overhead is absorbed by armor

raw.notes: SA3 cancel can only combo in high connect juggles, or if overhead is absorbed by armor

rows.name: Emergency Stop
rows.notes: Latest possible stop occurs after 41f (55f total animation); shortest Run~Stop distance: 1.64

raw.notes: Latest possible stop occurs after 41f (55f total animation); shortest Run~Stop distance: 1.64

rows.name: Arc Step
rows.notes: Can frame trap vs. 4f normals when canceled from 2HP, 5HP, or 5MP; only comes out if there is an opponent at close range on or near the ground after the 15th frame of Sprint; if opponent becomes airborne after startup, the whiffed attack has 1f active + 25f recovery; if Perfect Parried, Kimberly becomes airborne before opponent can Throw punish (can be punished with Drive Impact airborne connect or a Forced Knockdown normal up to 6f startup)

raw.notes: Can frame trap vs. 4f normals when canceled from 2HP, 5HP, or 5MP; only comes out if there is an opponent at close range on or near the ground after the 15th frame of Sprint; if opponent becomes airborne after startup, the whiffed attack has 1f active + 25f recovery; if Perfect Parried, Kimberly becomes airborne before opponent can Throw punish (can be punished with Drive Impact airborne connect or a Forced Knockdown normal up to 6f startup)

rows.name: Bushin Hojin Kick
rows.notes: KD Adv. is +31 if Arc Step is blocked, +30 on hit midscreen, +29 on hit in the corner

raw.notes: KD Adv. is +31 if Arc Step is blocked, +30 on hit midscreen, +29 on hit in the corner

rows.name: Bushin Izuna Otoshi
rows.notes: Grabs standing/crouching opponents, whiffs vs. airborne (can be backdashed)

raw.notes: Grabs standing/crouching opponents, whiffs vs. airborne (can be backdashed)

rows.name: Torso Cleaver
rows.notes: Kicking legs are Anti-Air Invuln 14-23f (cannot hit cross-up); active frames 3-7 only hit airborne opponents above Kimberly (only active frames 1-2 are useful for meaty pressure); 2nd hit will expand its hitbox if the 1st hit connects, allowing both to connect on hit/block; puts opponent into limited juggle state (launches high in juggles, allowing Drive Rush 2HP loops); () refers to higher KD advantage when juggled into

raw.notes: Kicking legs are Anti-Air Invuln 14-23f (cannot hit cross-up); active frames 3-7 only hit airborne opponents above Kimberly (only active frames 1-2 are useful for meaty pressure); 2nd hit will expand its hitbox if the 1st hit connects, allowing both to connect on hit/block; puts opponent into limited juggle state (launches high in juggles, allowing Drive Rush 2HP loops); () refers to higher KD advantage when juggled into

rows.name: Shadow Slide
rows.notes: 4-19f low profile and upper body projectile invuln (only on the Slide itself); puts opponent into limited juggle state; advantage varies significantly depending on which active frame of the 2nd hit connects; () refers to KD advantage when juggled into (KD +49 from 5MP~HP unless delayed); 1st hit forces stand vs. grounded opponents (ensures SA2 cancel can connect); if the 1st hit connects with the opponent, the 2nd hit has a greatly expanded vertical hitbox to ensure juggle consistency

raw.notes: 4-19f low profile and upper body projectile invuln (only on the Slide itself); puts opponent into limited juggle state; advantage varies significantly depending on which active frame of the 2nd hit connects; () refers to KD advantage when juggled into (KD +49 from 5MP~HP unless delayed); 1st hit forces stand vs. grounded opponents (ensures SA2 cancel can connect); if the 1st hit connects with the opponent, the 2nd hit has a greatly expanded vertical hitbox to ensure juggle consistency

rows.name: Neck Hunter
rows.notes: Puts airborne opponents into short OTG juggle state (only SA2/SA3 cancels can follow up); Super cancels whiff vs. grounded opponents

raw.notes: Puts airborne opponents into short OTG juggle state (only SA2/SA3 cancels can follow up); Super cancels whiff vs. grounded opponents

rows.name: Emergency Stop
rows.notes: Allows combo extensions from 5HP or 2HP, like a Drive Rush cancel for 2 bars with no damage scaling; latest possible stop occurs after 41f (54f total animation); shortest OD Run~Stop distance: 1.48

raw.notes: Allows combo extensions from 5HP or 2HP, like a Drive Rush cancel for 2 bars with no damage scaling; latest possible stop occurs after 41f (54f total animation); shortest OD Run~Stop distance: 1.48

rows.name: Arc Step
rows.notes: True blockstring when canceled from 2HP; can frame trap vs. 4f normals when canceled from 5HP, 5MP, 5MK, or 5MP~HP; only comes out if there is an opponent at close range on or near the ground after the 13th frame of OD Sprint; if opponent becomes airborne after startup, the whiffed attack has 1f active + 25f recovery; if Perfect Parried, Kimberly becomes airborne before opponent can Throw punish (can be punished with Drive Impact airborne connect or a Forced Knockdown normal up to 6f startup)

raw.notes: True blockstring when canceled from 2HP; can frame trap vs. 4f normals when canceled from 5HP, 5MP, 5MK, or 5MP~HP; only comes out if there is an opponent at close range on or near the ground after the 13th frame of OD Sprint; if opponent becomes airborne after startup, the whiffed attack has 1f active + 25f recovery; if Perfect Parried, Kimberly becomes airborne before opponent can Throw punish (can be punished with Drive Impact airborne connect or a Forced Knockdown normal up to 6f startup)

rows.name: Bushin Hojin Kick
rows.notes: Causes Wall Splat on hit near the corner; 2nd hit of the divekick occurs on hit only (1 hit on whiff or block); SA2 cancel works on either hit

raw.notes: Causes Wall Splat on hit near the corner; 2nd hit of the divekick occurs on hit only (1 hit on whiff or block); SA2 cancel works on either hit

rows.name: Bushin Izuna Otoshi
rows.notes: Throw connects against grounded opponents, whiffs vs. airborne or backdash; opponent is put into a limited juggle OTG state (slightly less time for juggle follow-ups than j.236PP)

raw.notes: Throw connects against grounded opponents, whiffs vs. airborne or backdash; opponent is put into a limited juggle OTG state (slightly less time for juggle follow-ups than j.236PP)

rows.name: Genius at Play
rows.notes: Refills 1 spray can; cannot reload if a spray can is currently stocked or is active on-screen

raw.notes: Refills 1 spray can; cannot reload if a spray can is currently stocked or is active on-screen

rows.name: Genius at Play
rows.notes: Refills 2 spray cans; cannot reload if a spray can is currently stocked or is active on-screen

raw.notes: Refills 2 spray cans; cannot reload if a spray can is currently stocked or is active on-screen

rows.name: Shuriken Bomb
rows.notes: Button strength determines distance; can throw a second can even while one is active on-screen (but cannot reload during this time); spraycan spawns on frame 18 (if Kimberly is hit before this, it will not be thrown); LP can hits the ground on frame 24 and explodes on frame 122; MP can hits the ground on frame 26 and explodes on frame 124; HP can hits the ground on frame 30 and explodes on frame 128; explosion can juggle if opponent is thrown immediately afterwards (MP/HP cans give more time for juggle follow-ups); puts opponent into free juggle state, or maintains the current juggle state if comboed into; important tool for corner oki scenarios; spraycan disappears if SA2 is activated

raw.notes: Button strength determines distance; can throw a second can even while one is active on-screen (but cannot reload during this time); spraycan spawns on frame 18 (if Kimberly is hit before this, it will not be thrown); LP can hits the ground on frame 24 and explodes on frame 122; MP can hits the ground on frame 26 and explodes on frame 124; HP can hits the ground on frame 30 and explodes on frame 128; explosion can juggle if opponent is thrown immediately afterwards (MP/HP cans give more time for juggle follow-ups); puts opponent into free juggle state, or maintains the current juggle state if comboed into; important tool for corner oki scenarios; spraycan disappears if SA2 is activated

rows.name: Shuriken Bomb Spread
rows.notes: Button strengths determine distance of each can (each strength of spraycan reaches the ground with the same timing as the 1-stock version); cannot reload while spraycans are active on-screen; spraycans spawn on frame 18 (if Kimberly is hit before this, they will not be thrown); LP+HP gives near-simultaneous explosions (6f gap between hits); LP+HP version can be useful in corner setups with Drive Impact that will break armor on the opponent's counter-DI; LP+MP and MP+HP versions result in a staggered explosion (explode on frames 122/170 or 124/174 respectively); staggered explosions combo on hit, and leave a blockstring gap of 19f for LP+MP and 20f for MP+HP; the staggered version generally gives stronger combos and allows for multiple mixups in one sequence; staggered version allows for extended blockstring sequences that can deal incredible Drive damage; many non-cinematic reversals can be punished after landing even when hitting Kimberly; the first explosion can juggle if opponent is thrown immediately afterwards; puts opponent into free juggle state, or maintains the current juggle state if comboed into; spraycan disappears if SA2 is activated; the 2nd can does not add any extra hits of damage scaling, but will take on the scaling level of any other hits that occur between the spraycan explosions (e.g. 1st can 70%, j.HP 60%, 2nd can 60%)

raw.notes: Button strengths determine distance of each can (each strength of spraycan reaches the ground with the same timing as the 1-stock version); cannot reload while spraycans are active on-screen; spraycans spawn on frame 18 (if Kimberly is hit before this, they will not be thrown); LP+HP gives near-simultaneous explosions (6f gap between hits); LP+HP version can be useful in corner setups with Drive Impact that will break armor on the opponent's counter-DI; LP+MP and MP+HP versions result in a staggered explosion (explode on frames 122/170 or 124/174 respectively); staggered explosions combo on hit, and leave a blockstring gap of 19f for LP+MP and 20f for MP+HP; the staggered version generally gives stronger combos and allows for multiple mixups in one sequence; staggered version allows for extended blockstring sequences that can deal incredible Drive damage; many non-cinematic reversals can be punished after landing even when hitting Kimberly; the first explosion can juggle if opponent is thrown immediately afterwards; puts opponent into free juggle state, or maintains the current juggle state if comboed into; spraycan disappears if SA2 is activated; the 2nd can does not add any extra hits of damage scaling, but will take on the scaling level of any other hits that occur between the spraycan explosions (e.g. 1st can 70%, j.HP 60%, 2nd can 60%)

rows.name: Bushin Thunderous Beats
rows.notes: Holding the button consumes 1 can for better damage and oki; travels ~75% screen length; spraycan only does 1 Drive damage to prevent opponent from recovering Drive meter too quickly

raw.notes: Holding the button consumes 1 can for better damage and oki; travels ~75% screen length; spraycan only does 1 Drive damage to prevent opponent from recovering Drive meter too quickly

rows.name: Neutral Taunt
rows.notes: "Man, come on, I'm ready to go!"; during SA3 install, this taunt can be input with 2PPPKKK instead

raw.notes: "Man, come on, I'm ready to go!"; during SA3 install, this taunt can be input with 2PPPKKK instead
